channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Operation Henhouse Nets Over 500 Arrests in UK Fraud Crackdown
item.description: UK police trumpet success of Operation Henhouse as they seize and freeze over £27m in suspected fraud proceeds

item.title: US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Aleksei Volkov has been sentenced to 81 months in prison for his role in Yanluowang ransomware attacks. The post US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Anthropic cuts action approval loop, lets Claude Code make the call
item.description: Auto mode is a new permissions feature in the Claude Code system that allows the AI to make approval decisions on a user’s behalf while safeguards review actions before execution. The feature is available on Team plan...

item.title: Malicious LiteLLM versions linked to TeamPCP supply chain attack
item.description: TeamPCP backdoored LiteLLM v1.82.7–1.82.8, likely via Trivy CI/CD, adding tools to steal credentials, move in Kubernetes, and keep persistent access. Threat actor TeamPCP compromised LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8...

item.title: Manager of botnet used in ransomware attacks gets 2 years in prison
item.description: A Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison after admitting that the phishing botnet he managed was used to launch BitPaymer ransomware attacks against 72 U.S. companies. [...]

item.title: Codenotary introduces AgentX for autonomous Linux infrastructure security
item.description: Codenotary has announced the availability of AgentX, an autonomous platform designed to manage, secure, and protect large-scale Linux infrastructure in the cloud or on-premises through coordinated networks of AI agent...

item.title: Spur Intelligence delivers deeper visibility into anonymized infrastructure
item.description: Spur Intelligence has announced new enhancements to its IP intelligence platform, designed to give security and fraud teams deeper visibility into anonymized infrastructure and enable informed, real-time decisions on...

item.title: HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach
item.description: The cybersecurity firm said the personal information of hundreds of employees was stolen in the hacker attack targeting Navia. The post HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach appeared first on Securit...

item.title: Tenable Hexa AI automates exposure management and security workflows
item.description: Tenable has revealed Tenable Hexa AI, the agentic AI engine of the Tenable One Exposure Management Platform that automates security workflows and transforms exposure intelligence into coordinated action to reduce cybe...

item.title: Barracuda strengthens cyber resilience with BarracudaONE platform updates
item.description: Barracuda Networks has announced advancements to the BarracudaONE cybersecurity platform and Barracuda Partner Success Program. The latest innovations strengthen cyber resilience across email, network access and gener...

item.title: Google’s TurboQuant cuts AI memory use without losing accuracy
item.description: Large language models carry a persistent scaling problem. As context windows grow, the memory required to store key-value (KV) caches expands proportionally, consuming GPU memory and slowing inference. A team at Googl...

item.title: HPE enhances security to support AI and distributed enterprise environments
item.description: HPE has unveiled new security innovations designed to help organizations scale distributed operations, reduce cyber risk, and maintain consistent governance as AI adoption accelerates across the enterprise. To help en...

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security. The action was designed...

item.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
item.description: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down.

item.title: RSA Conference: UK NCSC Head Urges Industry to Develop Vibe Coding Safeguards
item.description: The head of the UK’s NCSC is calling the cybersecurity industry to “seize the disruptive vibe coding opportunity” to make software more secure

item.title: FCC bans new routers made outside the USA over security risks
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has updated its Covered List to include all consumer routers made in foreign countries, banning the sale of new models in the U.S. [...]

item.title: DoE Publishes 5-Year Energy Security Plan
item.description: CESER’s Project Armor is a five year initiative to harden the US critical energy infrastructure, including strengthening energy systems ‘to prevent and recover from wildfires and other hazards’. The post DoE Publishes...

item.title: Data breach at Dutch Ministry of Finance impacts staff following cyberattack
item.description: Dutch Ministry of Finance disclosed a data breach affecting some employees following a cyberattack, investigation is ongoing. The Dutch Ministry of Finance disclosed a cyberattack detected on March 19 after a third-pa...

item.title: Three’s a Crowd: TeamPCP trojanizes LiteLLM in Continuation of Campaign
item.description: LiteLLM is the latest victim of TeamPCP’s open-source attack spree. Malicious versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 abuse Python’s .pth mechanism for stealthy persistence. The malware exfiltrates cloud credentials, CI/CD secrets...

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lat...

item.title: Silver Fox Cyber Campaigns Show Shift Toward Dual Espionage
item.description: Silver Fox pivots from ValleyRAT tax lures to WhatsApp‑style stealers, blending espionage & phishing

item.title: RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 1)
item.description: A summary of the announcements made by vendors on the first day of the RSAC 2026 Conference. The post RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 1) appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Citrix Urges Immediate Patching for Critical NetScaler Vulnerabilities
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Citrix’s NetScaler products allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak information from the appliance's memory

item.title: QualDerm Partners December 2025 data breach impacts over 3 Million people
item.description: Over 3.1M people affected as QualDerm Partners suffered a December 2025 breach, exposing personal, medical, and health insurance data. Over 3.1 million people are affected by a December 2025 data breach at QualDerm Pa...

item.title: GitHub 'OpenClaw Deployer' Repo Delivers Trojan Instead
item.description: An AI-assisted campaign is spreading more than 300 poisoned packages for diverse assets ranging from developer tools to game cheats.

item.title: New Npm 'Ghost Campaign' Uses Fake Install Logs to Hide Malware
item.description: Ghost npm campaign fakes install logs to steal sudo passwords and drop RATs that loot crypto and data

item.title: Zero Trust: Bridging the Gap Between Authentication and Trust
item.description: Passing MFA doesn't mean a session is safe, attackers can hijack tokens and bypass identity checks. Specops Software explains why Zero Trust must verify both user identity and device health. [...]

item.title: HackerOne discloses employee data breach after Navia hack
item.description: Bug bounty platform HackerOne is notifying hundreds of employees that their data was stolen after attackers hacked Navia, one of its U.S. benefits administrators. [...]

item.title: Former Ukrainian Foreign Minister Dmytro Kuleba to Address the New Cyber Frontline at Infosecurity Europe
item.description: Geopolitics and cyber warfare take center stage at Infosecurity Europe as Dmytro Kuleba discusses Ukraine’s hybrid war experience

item.title: Detecting IP KVMs, (Tue, Mar 24th)
item.description: I have written about how to&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;use IP KVMs securely, and recently, researchers at Eclypsium published yet another report on IP KVM vulnerabilities.&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;But there is another i...

item.title: Extortion Group Claims It Hacked AstraZeneca
item.description: The Lapsus$ hackers allegedly compromised internal code repositories, credentials, and employee data. The post Extortion Group Claims It Hacked AstraZeneca appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Infinite Campus warns of breach after ShinyHunters claims data theft
item.description: Infinite Campus, a widely used K-12 student information system, is warning customers of a data breach following an extortion attempt by a threat actor. [...]

item.title: Chrome 146 Update Patches High-Severity Vulnerabilities
item.description: The software refresh fixes eight memory safety bugs affecting seven Chrome components. The post Chrome 146 Update Patches High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: How a Large Bank Uses AI Digital Twins for Threat Hunting
item.description: JPMorgan Chase uses digital fingerprints and digital twins to spot online attackers and malicious behaviors while also reducing pesky false alerts.

item.title: Enterprise Cybersecurity Software Fails 20% of the Time, Warns Absolute Security
item.description: Poor patch management, increasingly complex IT environments and continued use of obsolete software puts organizations at risk from cyber threats, says the Absolute Security 2026 Resilience Risk Index

item.title: Yanluowang ransomware access broker gets 81 months in prison
item.description: A Russian national was sentenced to nearly 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to acting as an initial access broker (IAB) for Yanluowang ransomware attacks. [...]

item.title: Sandboxing AI agents, 100x faster
item.description: We’re introducing Dynamic Workers, which allow you to execute AI-generated code in secure, lightweight isolates. This approach is 100 times faster than traditional containers, enabling millisecond startup times for AI...

item.title: The Orca Approach to Runtime AI Security
item.description: The Runtime Gap: Why AI Security Can’t Stop at Posture Most AI security conversations in 2025 centered on posture. What models are deployed? Who has access? Are your AI pipelines misconfigured? These are the right que...

item.title: Citrix NetScaler critical flaw could leak data, update now
item.description: Citrix warns of a critical NetScaler flaw (CVE-2026-3055) that could leak sensitive data; users are urged to apply security updates immediately. Citrix issued security updates for two NetScaler vulnerabilities, includ...

item.title: North Korea-linked threat actors abuse VS Code auto-run to spread StoatWaffle malware
item.description: North Korea-linked threat actors use VS Code auto-run tasks to spread StoatWaffle malware via malicious projects that execute on folder open. North Korea-linked threat actor Team 8 behind the Contagious Interview camp...

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in conducting numerous atta...

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description: Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the application. The vulnerabil...

item.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
item.description: Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data.

item.title: Tool updates: lots of security and logic fixes, (Mon, Mar 23rd)
item.description: So, I&#;x26;#;39;ve been slow to get on the Claude Code/OpenCode/Codex/OpenClaw bandwagon, but I had some time last week so I asked Claude to review (/security-review) some of my python scripts. He found more than I&#...

item.title: IAM policy types: How and when to use them
item.description: June 3, 2022: Original publication date of this post. This post has been updated to add the additional IAM policy types: Resource control policies. You manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to A...

item.title: TeamPCP deploys Iran-targeted wiper in Kubernetes attacks
item.description: The TeamPCP hacking group is targeting Kubernetes clusters with a malicious script that wipes all machines when it detects systems configured for Iran. [...]

item.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run Tasks to Deploy StoatWaffle Malware
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the Contagious Interview campaign, also tracked as WaterPlum, have been attributed to a malware family tracked as StoatWaffle that's distributed via malicious Microsoft Visual Stu...

item.title: KICS GitHub Action Compromised: TeamPCP Strikes Again in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Checkmarx KICS scanner is the latest victim of a credential-stealing supply chain attack by TeamPCP. Between 12:58–16:50 UTC on March 23, 35 tags were hijacked. Learn how to audit your workflows, identify malicious ac...

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use...

item.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
item.description: A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection.

item.title: Pro-Iranian Nasir Security is targeting energy companies in the Gulf
item.description: Resecurity tracks Iran-linked Nasir Security targeting Middle East energy firms amid ongoing regional cyber and military threats. Resecurity (USA) is tracking a relatively new cybercriminal group called Nasir Security...

item.title: M-Trends 2026: Data, Insights, and Strategies From the Frontlines
item.description: Every year, the cyber threat landscape forces defenders to adapt to evolving adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs). In 2025, Mandiant observed a clear divergence in adversary pacing that closely aligns...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: CI/CD Backdoor, FBI Buys Location Data, WhatsApp Ditches Numbers & More
item.description: Another week, another reminder that the internet is still a mess. Systems people thought were secure are being broken in simple ways, showing many still ignore basic advisories. This edition covers a mix of issues: su...

item.title: Inside Gen 13: how we built our most powerful server yet
item.description: Cloudflare's Gen 13 servers introduce AMD EPYC™ Turin 9965 processors and a transition to 100 GbE networking to meet growing traffic demands. In this technical deep dive, we explain the engineering rationale behind ea...

item.title: Launching Cloudflare’s Gen 13 servers: trading cache for cores for 2x edge compute performance
item.description: Cloudflare’s Gen 13 servers double our compute throughput by rethinking the balance between cache and cores. Moving to high-core-count AMD EPYC ™ Turin CPUs, we traded large L3 cache for raw compute density. By runnin...

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and systems. That connectivity i...

item.title: Iran-linked actors use Telegram as C2 in malware attacks on dissidents
item.description: Iran-linked actors use Telegram as C2 to spread malware targeting dissidents and journalists, enabling surveillance and data theft. The FBI warns that Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) runs cyber cam...

item.title: Trivy Hack Spreads Infostealer via Docker, Triggers Worm and Kubernetes Wiper
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered malicious artifacts distributed via Docker Hub following the Trivy supply chain attack, highlighting the widening blast radius across developer environments. The last known cle...

item.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
item.description: Ten finalists will each have three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year.

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of accounts belonging to i...

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
item.description: The threat actors behind the supply chain attack targeting the popular Trivy scanner are suspected to be conducting follow-on attacks that have led to the compromise of a large number of npm packages with a previously...

item.title: CISA Flags Apple, Craft CMS, Laravel Bugs in KEV, Orders Patching by April 3, 2026
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, urging federa...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jumbo Flying Squid in the South Pacific
item.description: The population needs better conservation. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: AI Runtime Threat Detection: From Input to Real-World Impact
item.description: Understanding and detecting AI-driven behavior across model, workload, and cloud

item.title: Patch Now: Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
item.description: Attackers can execute arbitrary code without authentication if Oracle's Identity or Web Services Managers are exposed to the Web.

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
item.description: The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.

item.title: With Government's Role Uncertain, Businesses Unite to Combat Fraud
item.description: Major industry leaders agree to share information and collaborate to boost defenses in the wake of distressing online scams.

item.title: Native Launches With Security Control Plane for Multicloud
item.description: The cloud security startup's platform translates and enforces security policies across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle using provider-native controls.

item.title: Powering the agents: Workers AI now runs large models, starting with Kimi K2.5
item.description: Kimi K2.5 is now on Workers AI, helping you power agents entirely on Cloudflare’s Developer Platform. Learn how we optimized our inference stack and reduced inference costs for internal agent use cases.

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitiously harvest sens...

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 35 vulnerable drivers. EDR kill...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn’t work anymore but still do. Some o...

item.title: New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud. Perseus is built upo...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-20131 Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software and Cisco Sec...

item.title: Linux & Cloud Detection Engineering - Getting Started with Defend for Containers (D4C)
item.description: This technical resource provides a comprehensive walkthrough of Elastic’s Defend for Containers (D4C) integration, covering Kubernetes-based deployment, the analysis of BPF-enriched runtime telemetry, and the practica...

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) information techn...

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software. The vulnerabili...

item.title: SideWinder Espionage Campaign Expands Across Southeast Asia
item.description: The suspected India-linked threat group targets governments, telecom, and critical infrastructure using spear-phishing, old vulnerabilities, and rapidly rotating infrastructure to maintain persistent access.

item.title: Researchers: Meta, TikTok Steal Personal & Financial Info When Users Click Ads
item.description: Tracking pixels let social media companies spy on their users even after they click over to advertiser sites, gleaning credit card info, geolocations, and more, according to an analysis.

item.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevat...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-20963 Microsoft SharePoint Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-66376 Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) Cross-Site Scripting Vulner...

item.title: CISA Urges Endpoint Management System Hardening After Cyberattack Against US Organization
item.description: CISA is aware of malicious cyber activity targeting endpoint management systems of U.S. organizations based on the March 11, 2026 cyberattack against U.S.-based medical technology firm Stryker Corporation, which affec...

item.title: Claude Code Security and Magecart: Getting the Threat Model Right
item.description: When a Magecart payload hides inside the EXIF data of a dynamically loaded third-party favicon, no repository scanner will catch it – because the malicious code never actually touches your repo. As teams adopt Claude...

item.title: 9 Critical IP KVM Flaws Enable Unauthenticated Root Access Across Four Vendors
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned about the risks posed by low-cost IP KVM (Keyboard, Video, Mouse over Internet Protocol) devices, which can grant attackers extensive control over compromised hosts. The nine vuln...

item.title: Meta’s AI Glasses and Privacy
item.description: Surprising no one, Meta’s new AI glasses are a privacy disaster . I’m not sure what can be done here. This is a technology that will exist, whether we like it or not. Meanwhile, there is a new Android app that detects...

item.title: Product Walkthrough: How Mesh CSMA Reveals and Breaks Attack Paths to Crown Jewels
item.description: Security teams today are not short on tools or data. They are overwhelmed by both. Yet within the terabytes of alerts, exposures, and misconfigurations – security teams still struggle to understand context: Q: Which e...

item.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
item.description: Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.

item.title: Clear Communication: The Missing Link in Cybersecurity Success
item.description: When technical expertise meets clear communication, cybersecurity teams thrive. Learn how to foster trust and collaboration across diverse working groups.

item.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
item.description: Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.

item.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration and RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new method for exfiltrating sensitive data from artificial intelligence (AI) code execution environments using domain name system (DNS) queries. In a report publis...

item.title: LABScon25 Replay | Your Apps May Be Gone, But the Hackers Made $9 Billion and They’re Still Here
item.description: Andrew MacPherson exposes how crypto thieves exploit DeFi architecture, from the $1.5 billion Bybit heist to drainers-as-a-service and fund laundering.

item.title: AI is Everywhere, But CISOs are Still Securing It with Yesterday's Skills and Tools, Study Finds
item.description: A majority of security leaders are struggling to defend AI systems with tools and skills that are not fit for the challenge, according to the AI and Adversarial Testing Benchmark Report 2026 from Pentera. The report,...

item.title: South Korean Police Accidentally Post Cryptocurrency Wallet Password
item.description: An expensive mistake : Someone jumped at the opportunity to steal $4.4 million in crypto assets after South Korea’s National Tax Service exposed publicly the mnemonic recovery phrase of a seized cryptocurrency wallet....

item.title: Open, Closed and Broken: Prompt Fuzzing Finds LLMs Still Fragile Across Open and Closed Models
item.description: Unit 42 research unveils LLM guardrail fragility using genetic algorithm-inspired prompt fuzzing. Discover scalable evasion methods and critical GenAI security implications. The post Open, Closed and Broken: Prompt Fu...

item.title: Konni Deploys EndRAT Through Phishing, Uses KakaoTalk to Propagate Malware
item.description: North Korean threat actors have been observed sending phishing to compromise targets and obtain access to a victim's KakaoTalk desktop application to distribute malicious payloads to certain contacts. The activity has...

item.title: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
item.description: Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.

item.title: Get started with Elastic Security from your AI agent
item.description: Go from zero to a fully populated Elastic Security environment without leaving your IDE, using open source Agent Skills.

item.title: Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment
item.description: Iranian threat group Boggy Serpens' cyberespionage evolves with AI-enhanced malware and refined social engineering. Unit 42 details their persistent targeting. The post Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment appeared first o...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
item.description: Dozens of updated, malicious GlassWorm extensions have infested Open VSX, threatening software development supply chains.

item.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
item.description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.

item.title: GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projects — including Dj...

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization
item.description: The evolution of Iranian cyber operations in broad context: from custom wiper malware to misuse of legitimate admin tools and more. The post Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization ap...

item.title: Standing up for the open Internet: why we appealed Italy’s "Piracy Shield" fine
item.description: Cloudflare is appealing a €14 million fine from Italian regulators over "Piracy Shield," a system that forces providers to block content without oversight. We are challenging this framework to protect the Internet fro...

item.title: Researchers Warn of Global Surge in Fake Shipment Tracking Scams
item.description: Some of these campaigns are linked to Darcula, a Chinese-language phishing-as-a-service platform

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, and research that s...

item.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
item.description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.

item.title: CrackArmor Flaws Expose Linux Systems to Privilege Escalation
item.description: CrackArmor AppArmor flaws let local Linux users gain root, break containers and enable DoS attacks

item.title: Ransomware Under Pressure: Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures in a Shifting Threat Landscape
item.description: Written by: Bavi Sadayappan, Zach Riddle, Ioana Teaca, Kimberly Goody, Genevieve Stark Introduction Since 2018, when many financially motivated threat actors began shifting their monetization strategy to post-compromi...

item.title: Security Flaw in AWS Bedrock Code Interpreter Raises Alarms
item.description: DNS-based attack in AWS Bedrock AgentCore lets AI sandboxes exfiltrate cloud data

item.title: First Look: Orca’s RSAC 2026 Announcements
item.description: Over the past decade, protecting cloud-native applications has evolved. At first, security was reactive, driven by the traditional agent approach. Then came full cloud estate observability enabled by agentless technol...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-47813 Wing FTP Server Information Disclosure Vulnerability This type of vu...

item.title: Why Security Validation Is Becoming Agentic
item.description: If you run security at any reasonably complex organization, your validation stack probably looks something like this: a BAS tool in one corner. A pentest engagement, or maybe an automated pentesting product, in anothe...

item.title: FBI Calls for Help to Track Steam Malware Campaign
item.description: The FBI wants to hear from gamers who have downloaded Steam titles containing malware

item.title: UK: Companies House Web Glitch Exposes Corporate Details to Fraudsters
item.description: An issue with the Companies House website has put the personal and corporate information of millions at risk

item.title: Possible New Result in Quantum Factorization
item.description: I’m skeptical about—and not qualified to review—this new result in factorization with a quantum computer, but if it’s true it’s a theoretical improvement in the speed of factoring large numbers with a quantum computer.

item.title: Deploy AWS applications and access AWS accounts across multiple Regions with IAM Identity Center
item.description: If your organization relies on AWS IAM Identity Center for workforce access, you can now extend that access across multiple AWS Regions with multi-Region replication. Previously, AWS access portal was only available i...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m giving the Ross Anderson Lecture at the University of Cambridge’s Churchill College at 5:30 PM GMT on Thursday, March 19, 2026. I’m speaking at RSA...

item.title: Ivanti Connect Secure Zero-Day Vulnerability
item.description: What are the Vulnerabilities? Ivanti disclosed two vulnerabilities, CVE-2025-0282 and CVE-2025-0283, impacting Ivanti Connect Secure (“ICS”) VPN appliances. CVE-2025-0282 is an unauthenticated stack-based buffer overf...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.description: Some good news : squid stocks seem to be recovering in the waters off the Falkland Islands. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog modera...
